title: plantago sericea var. monticola
intro: Plantago sericea var. monticola, also known as mountain plantain, is a perennial herb belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in soothing skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The plant is rich in mucilages, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, making it beneficial for digestive health, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparation of mountain plantain can be taken as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and it is often used topically to treat minor cuts, burns, and skin conditions.
